title: Link a sub-item from board A to an item from board B via automations
excerpt:
Hello everyone, 
Do you know if it is possible when I create a subitem in board A for example, it automatically create a item in board B with same information ? 
thank you very much, 
Corentin

posts.author: corentin.dalfarra (AB testing)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello everyone,

Do you know if it is possible when I create a subitem in board A for example, it automatically create a item in board B with same information ?

thank you very much,

Corentin

posts.author: TRB-monday.com (Talia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @corentin.dalfarra,

Not possible at the moment, although it’s something we’re looking to do in the future! Keep checking custom automations, as we continue to add more support for subitems with those.

Feel free to write to us (at support@monday.com) as well in case we can give you more detailed support with your specific workflow.

posts.author: corentin.dalfarra (AB testing)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @TRB-monday.com

Thank you for your answer, I will wait for new product release then.

Have a great day,
Corentin

posts.author: Tudor (Tudor Costea)
posts.cookedHtml: Something I’d also be interested in!

posts.author: andrewalmand (Andrew Almand)
posts.cookedHtml:
@corentin.dalfarra @Tudor @TRB-monday.com

This is achievable now! You just have to trigger it with a Status change. See automation which supports subitems:
“When status changes to something, create an item in board”.

So define all of your subitem columns on board A, make sure they are present on board B and the fill out the automation. Have a status column in your subitems that says something like “Create” as a trigger and you should be all set.

What is still though, missing in the ability to link them via the “Connect Boards” column like you can with parent items. But if you just want to create a new item from a subitem Monday now allows that.

posts.author: JaneN (Jane Nichols)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

Can this be done so the new item takes the same description as the subitem I’ve created? the only way I can use this automation is to create a new item with a description of ‘New Item’

posts.author: andrewalmand (Andrew Almand)
posts.cookedHtml:
@JaneN

This should work with the following automation as an example:

image1213×179 7.45 KB

You need to choose the board first, then you can edit the default values of the item:

image1181×721 33.1 KB

Where is says “name” is the name of the subitem is being created from.

posts.author: JaneN (Jane Nichols)
posts.cookedHtml:
@andrewalmand thanks that’s worked!

Do you know how to also get other subitem columns brought across too? i.e not just the name? I have looked in the dropdown but am only given the options for the item columns, not the subitem columns

posts.author: andrewalmand (Andrew Almand)
posts.cookedHtml:
@JaneN

Not all columns are supported, but for example if you have a status in your Subitem that you want reflected on the new parent level item. First make sure the status options are the same, then just choose the supported inputs.

See example. You just have to scroll all they way down to the “Subitems supported fields”.

image1233×753 35.1 KB

posts.author: Admeirel (Adolpho Meirelles)
posts.cookedHtml:
My company really need for this feature/automation to support the ‘Connect Boards” column. What were aiming is a custom automation that would create an item in “board B” with a status change in a subitem from board A to a specific status, connecting the subitem in board A to the created item in board B.

@TRB-monday.com @monday-team

posts.author: Arielle (Arielle )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

When will this functionality be available?

For planning purposes, I am wanting to link subitems via the “Connect Boards” column, similar to parent items.

Is there a workaround for this?

posts.author: lledyard (Lyn Diaz)
posts.cookedHtml: We would also link this functionality to link subitems in addition to columns.

posts.author: Dani (Dani Lester)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Admeirel and @Arielle

I would recommend checking out our https://view.monday.com/1092205827-f4c654f1e91a8e4e76eedbb64150c940?r=use1 to see the upcoming releases and additions to the custom automations.

I do see that we are planning to add a number of triggers and actions around connecting boards in our ‘next up’ section, as well as the ability to trigger automations on the basis of a subitem being created.
You’ll find each of these releases have an anticipated release date, but it’s worth noting that these are subject to change.

It sounds like these additions could be a good option for you!

I hope this helps! Remember to mark as solved if this answers your question

Best,

Dani

posts.author: rohitbijlani (rohit bijlani)
posts.cookedHtml: Still awaiting a automation revolving around sub item and connect boards

posts.author: Cole (Cole Casper)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello @TRB-monday.com, I see this question was asked almost 2 years ago. When you say your team is looking into this feature for the future, is that 3-years time, 4-years time, 10-years time? I see many of these types of responses by your team on this community and have started to interpret it to mean: “Thank you for your question, but this is not a feature that will be implemented any time soon.”

Meytal

Screen Shot 2022-10-19 at 18.37.52702×616 30.8 KB

posts.author: arnoldprasad (Arnold P)
posts.cookedHtml: Is it possible to copy all sub-items from Item B when a new Item A is created in the same board but Item B is in Group B and Item A is in Group A? Item A is created via an automation. Any help would be appreciated.

posts.author: YashGarg (Yash Garg)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @arnoldprasad ,

We recently launched the app Special Automations - monday.com App Marketplace which has the required automation recipe for your use case.

image2338×380 50.2 KB

Also if you want any other automation, do let us know at support@superworkday.com

Thanks

posts.author: workflowboost (Bartosz @ Workflow Boost)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there,

Our Subitems Pro Automations app brings powerful automation recipes for automated linking between items and subitems. You may find the following one especially useful for you:

image1364×369 18.1 KB

Automation creates an item in selected board when subitem is created and connects them together. You can use mirror column to reflect the subitem data in a created item and vice versa.

You can also find automations to duplicate subitem to different parent items:

image1336×385 37.4 KB

If you have any questions or requests for custom automations, feel free to reach out to us!

posts.author: kevinfantasymedia (Kevin - Fantasy Media LTD)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @arnoldprasad

I know this is an old question, but just in case you’re looking for a reliable solution, check out this automation recipe on Subitem Automations!

image2196×654 31.7 KB

The app has many other automations that I’m sure you’ll find useful in your workflow.

posts.author: Fortimus_Team (Fortimus)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey,

You can try the Mirror Item Multiple Boards app.

We have a automations that can help you create items from subitems, and keep them fully synced.

Whenever anything changes on the subitem, it will automatically sync with the linked item on Board B.

Centralize your designs (7)1920×960 218 KB

Feel free to reach out to us at support@fortimus.co for any questions.

posts.author: WirkKarl (Karl)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey Corentin, yes, it’s possible! You can do this using a custom automation in Monday.com. Something like: When a subitem is created in Board A, create an item in Board B and map the fields you want. You might need to tweak it a bit depending on how many fields or updates you want synced.

posts.author: Fortimus_Team (Fortimus)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thats a great suggestion Karl.

However, the only bottleneck to that would be that the subitem is not linked to the item. So the changes on the original subitem won’t sync to the item once the item is created.

If you’re not looking for linking, then what Karl suggested is a good way.

If you’re looking for a complete sync functionality (updates/replies included), do try the Mirror item Multiple Boards app.

Feel free to reach out to us at support@fortimus.co for any questions or book a meeting with us directly.
